name: Brown Stock
recipeIngredient: 3 pounds veal or beef bones, sawed into 2-inch pieces
2 pounds stew beef, cut into 1 1/2 inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Spread the bones and beef in a flameproof roasting pan. Preheat the oven to 400°F. Roast for 25 minutes.
2. Add onions and carrots. Continue roasting, stirring once or twice, for another 30 minutes, or until well browned.
3. Transfer contents with a slotted spoon to a stockpot or kettle using a slotted spoon.
4. Pour off any fat from the pan. Add 2 cups water. Deglaze the pan over high heat by scraping up brown bits.
5. Add liquid to the pot with an additional 14 cups of cold water. Bring to a boil and skim the froth.
6. Reduce heat to medium and add 1/2 cup of cold water. Simmer, skimming the froth until tender (about 4 hours). For a more concentrated flavor, simmer longer until desired concentration is reached.
7. Add celery, garlic, parsley, salt, thyme, and bay leaf. Continue simmering until fully cooked.
8. Strain stock through a fine sieve into a bowl. Allow to cool to warm. Chill the stock and remove any remaining fat. Makes about 8 cups.
